creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Side note: 13.0-RELEASE is no longer supported; <https://www.freebsd.org/security/unsupported/>. 

From reboot(8) 
<https://www.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=reboot&sektion=8&manpath=FreeBSD>: 

>      -p      The system will turn off the power if it can.  If the power down 
>              action fails, the system will halt or reboot normally, depending on 
>              whether halt or reboot was called.


(In reply to Andrew Cagney from comment #0)

> …
> All buffers synced.
> Uptime: 37s
> uhub0: detached
> uhub1: detached


Re: <https://www.qemu.org/support/> I'll use Matrix to draw attention to your report. 

----

If I recall correctly, there's comparable behaviour – a visible end, not followed by closure of the window to the guest – with FreeBSD guests in VirtualBox hosted on FreeBSD, only if EFI is enabled at the guest level.
